info.title: mibvendor Permanently Free Public API
info.description: Permanently free, source-aware OID and bounded device-identity intelligence plus a fail-closed MIB source catalog. Direct PEN and reviewed organization-key fields avoid name-only correlation; unavailable keys stay null. The assessment endpoint accepts only four bounded identity signals, never a raw SNMP walk. There is no paid tier, billing, subscription, or paid quota upgrade. Free access is fair-use bounded, not unlimited use or an availability SLA. Raw files are served only for modules approved by file-specific or recognized pinned repository-license evidence; unapproved vendor sources remain directory-only.

200.description: Current process status; this is not uptime history or an SLA

405.description: Only GET and HEAD are allowed

head.summary: Check service status without a response body

200.description: Same status and no-store headers as GET, without a body

get.summary: Inspect deployed code, MIB data, and identity release identifiers

200.description: Deployed immutable identifiers and identity statistics

405.description: Only GET and HEAD are allowed

head.summary: Check version metadata without a response body

200.description: Same version and no-store headers as GET, without a body

get.summary: Inspect the active immutable data release

200.description: Release identity, effective publication view, bounded source catalog, and active-public statistics; clients must revalidate before reuse

post.summary: Assess bounded SNMP identity signals without accepting or retaining a raw walk
post.description: Submit only the minimum identity fields. Arrays, raw SNMP walks, and extra fields are rejected; there is no serial-number field, so do not place serials in text signals. sys_descr is processed in memory and is neither echoed nor part of the response evidence.

200.description: Evidence-separated assessment; conflicts remain explicit and body-dependent results are never shared-cacheable

c9300.summary: Exact C9300-24T assignment with direct PEN and reviewed organization key

post.summary: Resolve numeric OIDs while preserving order, duplicates, and instance suffixes

200.description: Per-item resolution results; body-dependent POST responses are never shared-cacheable

get.summary: Search by task, symbol, module, or numeric OID
Get structured syntax, access, status, description, relationships, and provenance
Get known ancestors, paginated direct children, and a bounded subtree summary for one object

200.description: Bounded lazy-tree navigation; the complete object corpus is never embedded in the response

get.summary: List rights-cleared MIB modules with provenance, checksums, and publication mode

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Start at 0, then pass the preceding response's non-null next_cursor unchanged. A null next_cursor marks the final page.

200.description: Paginated module catalog. Follow next_cursor exactly; do not derive it from counts.

nextPage.summary: Next page using the returned cursor

get.summary: Get one module's rights, source, checksums, dependency names, and raw availability
Download a deterministic bundle containing the exact raw MIB, retained license or notice, and provenance when redistribution is approved

200.description: Deterministic TAR containing the exact source MIB, retained license or generated notice, and provenance. The active unversioned route always revalidates publication controls.

schema.description: SHA-256 of the served TAR bytes.
SHA-256 of the exact MIB entry inside the TAR.

get.summary: List reviewed source families and their fail-closed publication modes
Inspect one source family's publication decision and scope evidence
Look up an IANA Private Enterprise Number

200.description: IANA registry assignment with identity caveat

enterprise.caveat: A PEN registration identifies the registry assignee; it does not prove device manufacturer, product model, ownership, or authenticity.

get.summary: Resolve a published exact sysObjectID claim or return its PEN boundary

200.description: Resolved, enterprise-only, or not-found result in the effective publication view; clients must revalidate before reuse

get.summary: Get direct, transitive, missing, and cyclic dependency states

CacheControl.description: Cache policy for this response; clients should reuse cacheable public results.

NoStore.description: Sensitive or live response data is not stored by shared or private caches.

NoCache.description: Clients may store the representation but must revalidate it so publication-control changes are not served stale.

ETag.description: Strong validator for the exact response bytes. Send it in If-None-Match on later GET requests.

RateLimitLimit.description: Current fair-use unit limit for the client window; this operational value may change.

RateLimitRemaining.description: Fair-use units remaining in the current client window.

RateLimitReset.description: Unix timestamp in seconds when the current client window resets.

RetryAfter.description: Seconds the client should wait before retrying a rate-limited request.

ObjectId.name: objectId
ObjectId.description: Stable module--symbol id or an exact numeric object OID; instance suffixes are not resolved by this item endpoint.

Problem.description: RFC 9457-compatible machine-distinguishable failure; Retry-After is emitted for rate-limit responses

FirmwareScope.description: The identity evidence establishes no firmware version, range, compatibility, or feature support; this is not an all-firmware claim.

repository_license_signal.description: Repository-level license signal; this does not override rights found in the source artifact.

artifact_rights.description: Rights classification of the referenced artifact itself.

scope.const: Live process self-check only; no uptime SLA or incident history.

sys_object_id_mappings.description: Unique exact sysObjectID keys enabled in the effective publication view.

exact_models.description: Exact-model claims, not a count of reviewed or unconditionally resolved models: 36 reviewed vendor-MIB normalizations plus up to 270 medium-confidence open-source project definitions in the active view.

vendor_identifiers.description: Exact vendor-MIB identifier assignments whose symbols may denote devices, chassis, modules, line cards, or components; these are not whole-device model claims.

project_observation_oids.description: Project-fixture observations are corroboration only, not universal model assignments.

project_definition_oids.description: Medium-confidence exact-model claims normalized from the pinned RackTables static mapping after structural, privacy, and source-conflict quarantine. Reviewed material disagreements with project observations still return ambiguous results.

project_identity_oid_coverage.description: Unique union of active project observations and publishable open-source project definitions.

sys_object_id.description: Numeric OID with uint32 arcs; one leading dot is accepted.

ent_physical_vendor_type.description: Numeric ENTITY-MIB vendor-type OID with uint32 arcs; one leading dot is accepted.

sys_descr.description: Processed in memory for bounded platform signatures; never echoed, stored, or returned as evidence.

organization_key.description: Reviewed stable organization key when available; null is explicit and must not be inferred from a name.

mib_identifier.description: Vendor MIB symbol when the source supplies one; this is not automatically a whole-device model.

firmware_scope.description: Null only when no singular identity result exists (unknown or conflicting evidence).

object_count.description: Compatibility alias for statistics.oid_nodes.searchable_records; this is not the catalog OID-node count.

CorpusStatistics.description: Counts derived only from the active public release. Candidate, staged, and quarantined content is not included.

catalog_oid_nodes.description: OID-bearing records parsed from the active module catalog.

supplemental_legacy_records.description: Legacy fixtures duplicated by the active catalog are removed from the searchable release.

searchable_records.description: Catalog OID nodes plus non-duplicated supplemental legacy records.

active_module_definitions.description: TEXTUAL-CONVENTION definitions in active redistributable module artifacts.

searchable_records.description: Textual conventions are not yet first-class searchable records.

enterprise_records.description: IANA PEN registry records; these are registry assignments, not device identities.

sys_object_id_mappings.description: Published exact sysObjectID mappings in the effective publication view. Exact OID match remains separate from claim strength.

exact_models.description: Exact-model claim count; not a reviewed-model or guaranteed-resolution count.

PublicationControlStatistics.description: Effective fail-closed source and module withdrawal state for this application release.

next_cursor.description: Pass a non-null value unchanged as the next request's cursor. Null marks the final page.

source_license.description: Legacy rights-cleared built-in source license field.

rights.description: Rights statement for a rights-cleared built-in source.

repository_license_signal.description: License detected for the containing repository; it is not the rights classification of the referenced MIB artifact.

enum: independently-authored factual metadata; artifact rights deny raw redistribution
RackTables static exact key with mibvendor-authored bounded model-label normalization

mib_identifier.description: Vendor MIB symbol; its presence does not by itself assert a whole-device model.

claim_strength.description: Strength of the identity assertion. An exact OID match does not imply an exact-model claim.

firmware_scope.description: Null only when no singular identity result exists (unknown or conflicting evidence).

next_cursor.description: Pass a non-null value unchanged as the next request's cursor. Null marks the final page.
